persona: Alex, Node.js dev, has used task/notes/timewarrior, 20-min budget, has not seen the code

S1_cold_install.notes: pnpm install was cached, build 1s, test 2.7s (431/431 pass). npm install fails as documented; TROUBLESHOOTING.md advice is wrong.

S2_first_impression.notes: All five discovery commands clean, sub-150ms each.

S3_config_init.notes: doctor FAIL with clear fix message; profile set rejects key=value; profile show returns defaults as if real.

S4_happy_path.notes: README quickstart works end-to-end; RAG returns same chunk 4x (de-dup bug); ask output noisy (internal prompt + stub).

S5_edge_case.notes: Unicode OK, decimals OK, multi-word category OK; empty bodies show only usage, journal find shows wrong usage, no max-amount cap, no max-body warning.

S6_secondary.notes: rotate-secret is documented but does not exist; companion server exposes only /health; serve exposes only /health and /completion; metrics are empty; redact doesn't read stdin; multimodal dumps the whole file.

S7_resume.notes: seed 10000 finishes in 1.5s; state persists; seed is idempotent with clear 'skipped N' message.

S8_error_paths.notes: 15 sabotages tested. seed cap error is best-in-class. profile set with --name=Alex silently stores a literal --name key. rotate-secret fails because it does not exist.

S9_resource_limits.notes: seed 100k in 1.5s, peak RSS ~103MB (Apple Silicon overestimate; actual ~13MB), no hangs.

S10_readme_reality_check.notes: 5+ README examples work for quickstart. P2P example falls through to local stub without contacting peer. v3 web UI pitch does not work end-to-end.

web_ui_smoke.notes: CORS preflight blocked even on /health; /chat and /journal return 404; chat box shows 'error: Failed to fetch' for every message.

findings.title: profile set name=Alex rejected; the CLI requires two separate args
findings.expected: Either the key=value form works, or the usage line shows an example.
findings.actual: usage: edgewell profile set <key> <value>
findings.suggestedFix: Accept key=value as a single arg, or document the space form in the usage line and show an example.

findings.title: profile show on a fresh install returns the default profile as if it were the user's
findings.repro: rm -f data/profile.json && node bin/edgewell.js profile show
findings.expected: (no profile on disk) or empty defaults.
findings.actual: Populated JSON with name:"friend", default health/finance goals, baseline numbers, createdAt:null.
findings.suggestedFix: If the file does not exist, return null and have callers render '(no profile)'.

findings.title: profile init is advertised as a wizard but silently writes the defaults with no prompts
findings.expected: Either an interactive wizard (when a TTY is present) or a clear message that the wizard is unavailable.
findings.actual: 'wrote default profile (no TTY; pass a TTY to run the wizard)' is printed even when no wizard exists.
findings.suggestedFix: Implement the wizard for TTY runs, or change the message to 'wrote default profile (no interactive prompts available in this mode)'.

findings.title: rag search returns the same chunk 4x - RAG results are not de-duplicated
findings.repro: rag ingest data/sample_health_notes.txt (already indexed) then rag search 'how much water'
findings.expected: At most one row per unique chunk id.
findings.actual: The Nutrition chunk from sample_health_notes.txt is listed 4 times with identical score 0.008.
findings.suggestedFix: De-duplicate by chunk id (or hash) in the search response formatter.

findings.title: Empty / whitespace inputs to ask, rag search, journal add produce just a usage: line with no specific reason
findings.expected: 'question cannot be empty' / 'text cannot be empty'
findings.actual: usage: edgewell ask "<question>" (and equivalents)
findings.suggestedFix: Print the specific reason before the usage line.

findings.title: journal find <tag> (documented alias for journal-by-tag) prints the wrong usage line on bad input
findings.expected: usage: edgewell journal find <tag> (or similar)
findings.actual: usage: edgewell journal <add|list> -- <find> is missing from the printed list
findings.suggestedFix: Fix the usage line for the journal subcommand to enumerate add, list, find, tail, rm, restore, etc.

findings.title: rotate-secret is listed in docs/COMMANDS.md but does not exist as a command
findings.expected: The command runs (rotate companion secret).
findings.actual: unknown command: rotate-secret
findings.suggestedFix: Either implement the command (it pairs with `token`) or remove it from COMMANDS.md.

findings.title: redact does not read from stdin despite being a natural pipe target
findings.repro: echo 'my email is j@x.com' | node bin/edgewell.js redact
findings.expected: The redacted text.
findings.actual: usage: edgewell redact <text> [...more words] ... (no stdin read)
findings.suggestedFix: Read from stdin if no positional arg is given; document the convention in the help line.

findings.title: The companion HTTP server (v3.0.0 headlined feature) implements only GET /health. All other advertised routes return 404.
findings.repro: (1) start edgewell companion --port N; (2) curl http://127.0.0.1:N/v1/journal (with or without token)
findings.expected: The companion server should expose /v1/chat, /v1/journal, /v1/agents, /v1/plan, /v1/ask (as the README/web UI imply).
findings.actual: {"error":"not found","method":"POST","path":"/v1/journal"} for every v1 route
findings.suggestedFix: Wire the existing internal Router (used in the unit tests) to the public companion server, or document the actual route names.

findings.title: web/app.js fetches /chat, /journal, /journal?limit=20 from the companion - none of these endpoints exist
findings.repro: Open web/index.html while a companion is running on port 8787, type 'Hello', submit
findings.expected: The chat shows a reply from EdgeWell.
findings.actual: The UI displays 'error: Failed to fetch' for every message; the journal list stays empty.
findings.suggestedFix: Either implement /chat and /journal on the companion server, or change the web UI to call the (working) /v1/* equivalents.

findings.title: docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md recommends `npm install --legacy-peer-deps` to fix the `npm install` failure. The actual error is the `link:` URL type, not a peer-dep issue.
findings.expected: Either `npm install --legacy-peer-deps` succeeds, or the docs say 'use pnpm'.
findings.actual: npm error code EUNSUPPORTEDPROTOCOL. `--legacy-peer-deps` does not help.
findings.suggestedFix: Update TROUBLESHOOTING.md: 'EdgeWell's @qvac/sdk is a link: dependency; only pnpm is supported. Use pnpm.'

findings.title: ask output is noisy: the entire prompt template (with RAG context inline) is printed before the stub completion
findings.repro: node bin/edgewell.js ask 'How can I save 20% of my income?'
findings.expected: A clean one-paragraph answer (or, for the stub, just the route + a friendly placeholder).
findings.actual: The full RAG context, the user prompt, the agent template, then [stub completion for: ...]
findings.suggestedFix: In stub mode, suppress the internal prompt and print something like '[stub] route=finance - ask: "How can I save 20%..."'.

findings.title: A 10 KB journal body is accepted with no warning; no max-length check
findings.expected: Either truncated, or a soft warning.
findings.actual: logged, no message.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a soft cap (e.g. 4 KB) with a hint to split long entries.

findings.title: Companion server does not send Access-Control-Allow-Origin, so even GET /health (the only working route) is blocked by CORS preflight from a different-origin browser client
findings.repro: (1) start edgewell companion; (2) open the bundled web/index.html from a python3 -m http.server
findings.expected: The page shows 'connected - v3.0.0 - 6 agents'.
findings.actual: The page shows 'journal error: Failed to fetch'; console shows 4x 'blocked by CORS policy'.
findings.suggestedFix: Add CORS middleware to the companion Router (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * for the bundled UI, or echo the request Origin).

findings.title: The P2P client (EDGEWELL_P2P_ENABLED=1 ...) never actually contacts the peer - it always returns the local stub
findings.repro: (1) start serve --port 18787 on the same host; (2) EDGEWELL_P2P_ENABLED=1 EDGEWELL_P2P_HOST=127.0.0.1 EDGEWELL_P2P_PORT=18787 edgewell ask 'hi'
findings.expected: The response is streamed from the peer.
findings.actual: [lifestyle] [stub completion for: hi] (the peer is never contacted; the same response appears even when the host:port is unreachable)
findings.suggestedFix: Trace the client path: createEdgeWell builds a PeerClient only when p2p.enabled is true; the in-process DelegatingLLM should be the one making the call. Verify that EDGEWELL_P2P_ENABLED=1 actually flips the flag (env-var name and parse path).

findings.title: metrics shows '(no metrics recorded yet)' after exercising the CLI for several minutes
findings.repro: run info, doctor, seed 5, rag search 'test', then metrics
findings.expected: Some counters incremented.
findings.actual: All counters empty.
findings.suggestedFix: Either wire inc() calls into the dispatch hot path, or remove the `metrics` command from the v3 advertised surface.

findings.title: profile set --name=Alex is treated as a literal key, not a flag - the -- prefix is silently consumed
findings.expected: Either rejected as a flag-looking key, or --name is parsed as a flag.
findings.actual: Silently sets the key `--name` with the value `Alex` (a key that begins with `--` is unlikely to round-trip).
findings.suggestedFix: If a key starts with `-`, reject it with 'key cannot start with `-`'.

findings.title: multimodal <file> dumps the entire ingested file to stdout on success, mixing ingest status with file contents
findings.expected: A short 'indexed N bytes, kind=text' line.
findings.actual: A 70-line dump of the file body, intermixed with the status banner.
findings.suggestedFix: Move the dump behind --print or --verbose; default to the short status line.

findings.title: vector search returns rows with score=0.0000 - i.e. cosine exactly zero - without explaining what that means
findings.expected: Hide zero-score rows, or label them as '(no signal)'.
findings.actual: Three of five rows have score=0.0000 with no annotation.
findings.suggestedFix: Filter zero-score rows from the output, or rename to 'score=0.0 (no match)'.

findings.title: import of an already-imported file says 'imported 0 journal entries, 0 expenses' - no indication that records were recognised as duplicates
findings.expected: 'imported 0 new (skipped 7 existing)' or similar.
findings.actual: 'imported 0 journal entries, 0 expenses'
findings.suggestedFix: Include a duplicate-skip count in the output.

findings.title: models describe medpsy-1 returns all fields as 'unknown' - the help does not show the canonical id format
findings.expected: Helpful error: 'no model with id medpsy-1; try `edgewell models list`'
findings.actual: {"id":"medpsy-1","family":"unknown",...}
findings.suggestedFix: The unknown-id path should be a 'not found' error, not a placeholder record.

findings.title: profiles apply tinkerer prints '(writes to ~/.edgewell/profile.json in a future release)' - the user just ran a command and the command itself admits it doesn't persist
findings.expected: The applied profile sticks across runs.
findings.actual: Apparent confirmation, with a note that the change is not actually saved.
findings.suggestedFix: Either persist the selection (e.g. write to data/profile.json or ~/.edgewell/state.json) or remove the misleading success message.

findings.title: doctor reports a single FAIL but counts it as '1 check(s) failed' in the summary - the wording is plural for a count of one
findings.expected: '1 check failed'
findings.actual: '1 check(s) failed'
findings.suggestedFix: Use proper singular/plural ('1 check failed' / 'N checks failed').

findings.title: No upper cap on expense add - a 1,000,000,000 expense is accepted silently
findings.expected: Either an upper cap (with a clear error), or a soft warning.
findings.actual: logged 1000000000 (car) and the weekly summary then shows total 1000000418.00
findings.suggestedFix: Add a configurable max amount (the schema validator already rejects > EXPENSE_MAX per the unit tests, but the CLI does not surface that check).
